gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/404)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/404)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَ يَتَجَرَّدُ الرَّجُلُ عِنْدَ صَبِّ الْمَاءِ تُرَى عَوْرَتُهُ أَوْ يُصَبُّ عَلَيْهِ الْمَاءُ أَوْ يَرَى هُوَ عَوْرَةَ النَّاسِ فَقَالَ كَانَ أَبِي يَكْرَهُ ذَلِكَ مِنْ كُلِّ أَحَدٍ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Aban Bin Usman, from Ibn Abu Yafour who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Can the man be bare during the pouring of the water to see his own private parts, or the water is poured over him, or he see the private parts of the people?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘My<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> disliked that from every one’.
en.sarwar: 27. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from more than one person who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Looking at the private parts of non-Muslims is like looking at the private parts of donkeys.’”
